127740.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants a service-oriented, transparent, and future-proof organization; diversity is taken seriously and supported but does not impose a strict 50/50 norm. In our view, more women than men (or vice versa) could be part of the new executive board. A good mix strengthens teams, and at the same time, substantive and specialist knowledge is important for the best results for residents. Indeed, more women than men could be included in the new council.

127744.explanation: Provided it fits the landscape. The agricultural sector is the backbone of the rural area; entrepreneurs must be given space as long as the agricultural and landscape character is preserved.

127750.explanation: Neutral. Lokaal Belang wants clean air and policies that do not negatively impact public health, with attention to conscious and responsible heating behavior.

127752.explanation: Lokaal Belang cherishes traditions, events, and activities that connect people. There might possibly be consideration for several central fireworks shows at various locations in the municipality, but this does not say anything about municipal funding for them.

127753.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants less waste and litter and promotes reuse. The more facilities provided, the more expensive it becomes. Lokaal Belang is against unnecessary tax increases for residents.

127754.explanation: Lokaal Belang supports festivals, culture, and music as unifying forces but also guards liveability and health; our program does not contain specific stricter standards for festival noise.

127757.explanation: Lokaal Belang believes everyone should be able to participate in sports, regardless of disability or income; the municipality supports future-proof sports opportunities and removing financial barriers, including through the Gelrepas.

127759.explanation: Lokaal Belang agreed with the Integral Housing Plan to realize good school buildings and wants sustainable, appropriate educational facilities in all neighborhoods and villages.

127764.explanation: Feeling safe and prevention are central; the municipality must be visibly present in neighborhoods and villages and actively focus on enforcement and social safety. Much can be done in other ways besides using enforcement officers.

127765.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants a safe Zevenaar and tackles crime. Cameras are not mentioned in our election program. The deployment of resources must balance privacy and liveability. We are open to camera surveillance.

127766.explanation: Lokaal Belang pays attention to femicide, social safety, and preventing people from falling through the cracks; good shelter and protection are necessary.

127767.explanation: Those with a local connection to Zevenaar, the city, neighborhood, and villages should get priority in housing; Lokaal Belang wants residents of the municipality to have priority for housing.

127771.explanation: Lokaal Belang emphasizes self-direction and participation but also low-threshold, appropriate support. There is no general mandatory reciprocity requirement.

127773.explanation: Lokaal Belang focuses on helping people become financially independent and wants support to be accessible; expanding income limits can prevent people from being left out. Together with the resident, underlying reasons are investigated to tailor the help accordingly.

127775.explanation: The municipality encourages sustainability and a future-proof mobility policy; facilitating electric driving through charging points fits a cleaner living environment.

127776.explanation: Traffic safety is a priority, and the feeling of safety in traffic is important; Lokaal Belang wants research into 30 km/h zones and a traffic-safe center.

127777.explanation: The municipality must trust professionals and provide appropriate care; customization and respect for the individual situation and background of residents are essential principles.

127781.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants solid finances and a strong recreational offering; rates may only increase if truly necessary and the tourist sector can bear it.

127783.explanation: The local economy and vibrant city are important; entrepreneurs must be given space, and residents must be able to recreate and shop in the city center and village centers.

127784.explanation: We house status holders (people with residence permits) as much as possible in transition locations and temporary housing, so pressure on the regular social housing stock remains limited. Ukrainian refugees currently live in buildings owned by the municipality.

127791.explanation: Safety and liveability are leading, so decision-making about a coffeeshop requires careful local consideration. We remain cautious.

127792.explanation: The program states that at least 30% of newly built homes must be social rental housing, with income requirements realistic to be able to rent. Social rental homes must be well maintained. Allocation of social rental homes goes to residents with regional ties.

127793.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants sufficient housing for all target groups and careful use of space. High-rise flats must be assessed per location. If it is not possible there, we will not place them there.

127794.explanation: Strong, liveable village centers with facilities and meeting places are a priority; village centers contribute to connection and liveability in smaller communities.

127795.explanation: Parking policy must be appropriate to the location and situation and therefore continuously monitored and adjusted if necessary. Lokaal Belang has always taken a pragmatic approach based on concrete situations to parking problems and will continue to do so.

127796.explanation: Lokaal Belang wants good accessibility to the center and facilities but also an attractive and safe center; additional parking spaces must be carefully weighed.

127798.explanation: Lokaal Belang supports municipal reorganization with Doesburg, Duiven, and Westervoort, provided the process is handled carefully and facilities and identity are preserved.

127800.explanation: The municipality of Zevenaar meets the national quota of the Spread Act. Several locations already exist for family reunification migrants. Additional space is currently not required. Lokaal Belang is pleased with the national plans to better distribute family reunification migrants.

127740.explanation: Diversity is important, but a fixed 50/50 standard does not always fit and should therefore not be a goal in itself. We choose the best aldermen based on quality and actively aim for broad representation.

127744.explanation: CDA Zevenaar wants to give farmers space for side activities such as a shop or camping. This can contribute to a vital rural area and a fair income. However, we set conditions: it must fit the environment, cause no nuisance, and respect nature, traffic, and local residents.

127750.explanation: We do not ban things arbitrarily, but health comes first. Burning can only be responsible: no nuisance, clean burning, and when air quality is poor, we must be able to limit it. Under these conditions, the use of fireplaces and wood stoves should simply remain possible.

127752.explanation: Fireworks on New Year's Eve is a valuable Dutch tradition that brings people together. CDA Zevenaar believes the municipality should, in consultation with neighborhood and village councils, promote a central fireworks show per community. This increases safety, support, and solidarity within our communities.

127753.explanation: Free home collection is expensive and encourages littering. We are satisfied with our accessible waste disposal stations and support customized solutions for those who really cannot arrange it themselves.

127754.explanation: Events are part of a vibrant municipality, but neighbors must be able to sleep. Noise standards are general rules that often suffice but not always. Therefore, we choose neighborhood participation and tailored solutions per location, clear end times, and better enforcement against nuisance.

127757.explanation: Sports are important for health and social connection. With the Gelrepas, Zevenaar already makes sports participation possible for people with low incomes, including those on social assistance. CDA Zevenaar supports this scheme and wants everyone to be able to participate easily.

127759.explanation: Good education starts with good and safe school buildings. Investments are needed in the coming years. CDA Zevenaar invests specifically where the need is greatest, in future-proof and energy-efficient schools in all communities. This is an investment in our children and therefore money well spent.

127764.explanation: Safety is important, but more enforcement officers (BOAs) is not a goal in itself. CDA Zevenaar chooses targeted deployment where needed, focusing on prevention and cooperation with the police. Where possible, we seek smart and efficient alternatives to increase safety and livability.

127765.explanation: Everyone must be able to feel safe in Zevenaar. Camera surveillance can help, especially in places with nuisance or insecurity. We are not in favor of unnecessary monitoring but deploy cameras in a targeted and careful manner, with clear rules, privacy protection, and regular evaluation.

127766.explanation: Domestic violence is unacceptable and everyone deserves a safe home. Shelter must always be available. We prefer to organize this regionally and professionally. If a facility is needed in Zevenaar, we will cooperate, ensuring good accessibility and aftercare.

127767.explanation: CDA Zevenaar wants to build for local residents. If starters are forced to leave and elderly cannot find suitable housing, communities weaken their identity and social cohesion. Therefore, local ties may play a role in the allocation of social rental homes, within applicable rules.

127771.explanation: Participation is important for people and society. CDA Zevenaar finds it reasonable that a social assistance benefit includes an appropriate return. This requires customization: we encourage socially meaningful work and volunteering, with good guidance and without unreasonable coercion.

127773.explanation: CDA Zevenaar wants to help people in poverty but does not opt to raise income limits. We focus on tailored solutions, prevention, and targeted support via Zevenaar Debt-Free. This way, we help those in need, prevent poverty traps, and keep the care and social domain affordable.

127775.explanation: CDA Zevenaar supports electric driving but is cautious about adding extra parking spaces as charging spots. That reduces scarce parking space. We encourage charging at logical locations, monitor the growth of electric driving, and seek a good balance between sustainability and accessibility.

127776.explanation: We do not find a general 30 km/h limit within built-up areas desirable. However, we do see that lower speeds in residential neighborhoods and courtyards increase safety. Additionally, we support discouraging motorized traffic where possible for a healthier lifestyle and less emissions.

127777.explanation: CDA Zevenaar believes care must correspond to who people are and what they stand for. At the same time, it is not always feasible to guarantee full choice for everyone. Quality, accessibility, and continuity of care come first, with attention to religion and worldview where possible.

127781.explanation: CDA Zevenaar wants to stimulate tourism and recreation because they contribute to vibrant communities and the local economy. A higher tourist tax deters entrepreneurs and visitors. We opt for reasonable charges and an attractive climate for guests and entrepreneurs.

127783.explanation: CDA Zevenaar believes that shopping Sundays are not a goal in themselves. The initiative should come from entrepreneurs, not the municipality. Decisions about extra shopping Sundays should be made in consultation with entrepreneurs and residents, respecting peace, freedom of choice, and differences per community.

127784.explanation: CDA Zevenaar sees that the shortage of social rental homes for local residents is significant. Therefore, the construction of social rental housing must increase substantially. Refugees with a residence permit should not automatically receive priority; allocation must be fair and do justice to everyone who has been waiting a long time.

127791.explanation: CDA Zevenaar opts for a zero policy: no coffee shops. They do not contribute to a healthy lifestyle and increase risks of addiction and mental health issues. We protect vulnerable youth and focus on prevention, distance from schools and clubs, and strict enforcement.

127792.explanation: We want to build for our own residents and tailor supply to local needs. This means sufficient affordable housing. Thirty percent social rental is a good guideline, provided there remains room for starter homes and mid-range rental. We make firm regional agreements on this.

127793.explanation: CDA Zevenaar sees that high-rise buildings contribute to housing close to facilities and space saving. At the same time, we understand concerns about livability. Therefore, we opt for customization: first add a few houses and only build high-rise in appropriate locations, matching the scale and appearance of the environment.

127794.explanation: Villages need a heart. We invest in village halls and centers as places for meeting, clubs, and nearby care—fitting the scale of each community.

127795.explanation: We want an accessible and lively center but do not find it responsible to generally lower paid parking fees. Rates must remain fair and cost-covering.

127796.explanation: CDA Zevenaar sees parking pressure, but more parking spots in the center are not always desirable or feasible. Extra asphalt reduces livability and space. Therefore, we choose smart parking, clear parking standards for new construction, and promote shared cars where effective.

127798.explanation: Cooperation in the Liemers region is important, but a merger is neither an obligation nor a goal in itself. Zevenaar chooses to merge only if research shows clear added value and there is broad support among residents and neighboring municipalities, preserving facilities and proximity in the local communities.

127800.explanation: CDA Zevenaar believes our municipality’s shelter capacity has limits. The focus must be on good housing and integration of people already here. Additional temporary shelter for family reunification places too much pressure on housing, care, and facilities and undermines local support.

127740.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

127744.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

127750.explanation: We are not for a total ban but want it to be possible to prohibit wood burning in places where it causes nuisance.

127752.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

127753.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

127754.explanation: If there is nuisance or possible hearing damage, noise standards must be adjusted.

127757.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

127759.explanation: A good school requires a good building. It is the municipality’s responsibility to provide school buildings where safe and pleasant learning is possible. Investing in that is investing in our future.

127764.explanation: Safety is very important and transfer (overslag) must be minimized as much as possible. Extra enforcement officers (BOAs) can help with that.

127765.explanation: Camera surveillance can help increase safety. But it also invades your privacy. Therefore, we believe camera surveillance should only be (temporarily) expanded when there is a concrete reason.

127766.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A shelter for domestic violence victims is indispensable for our municipality.

127767.explanation: It is important that people who grew up in our municipality can continue to live there. Increasingly, we hear from young people and starters that it is impossible to find a home in their own village or neighborhood.

127771.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

127773.explanation: Poverty is a major problem; even in Zevenaar, there are more working poor. They also deserve support where needed, so, for example, their children can go to sports clubs.

127775.explanation: There are more electric cars, but the need for extra charging points varies greatly per village or neighborhood. Charging spots often take away ordinary parking spaces. Therefore, the municipality should only add extra charging points if requested by the neighborhood.

127776.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

127777.explanation: When you need care, you also want it from someone you know and who understands you. Therefore, it is important that, where possible, people can choose their own caregiver.

127781.explanation: No one is in favor of high taxes. But if choices must be made, we are willing to increase the tourist tax because tourists also use the municipality’s facilities and should make a fair contribution.

127783.explanation: We are not against shopping Sundays, but entrepreneurs and employees also have the right to a free weekend. However, if research shows sufficient demand and support for extra shopping Sundays, we are not opposed.

127784.explanation: Everyone with a large distance to the housing market or in urgent need must be able to get priority for a social rental home. This includes refugees.

127791.explanation: Only if good agreements are made with the surroundings and nuisance can be minimized as much as possible.

127792.explanation: The housing shortage is high. Affordable houses are the best solution for this.

127793.explanation: The housing shortage is one of the biggest problems at the moment. High-rise buildings or apartment complexes can be a solution to the lack of affordable housing.

127794.explanation: This is the ideal image, but that can only happen with the help of entrepreneurs and residents. The municipality cannot enforce a vibrant village center, but can help enable it.

127795.explanation: Nobody supports expensive paid parking. But (parking) space is scarce and, for example, building a parking garage is expensive. It is therefore only logical that users pay for it.

127796.explanation: For new (construction) projects in and around the center of Zevenaar, parking options must be carefully considered and expanded where necessary.

127798.explanation: We face major challenges. More expertise and decisiveness are needed for that. As a larger municipality, that is possible. However, this must not come at the expense of service delivery or the identity of our villages and neighborhoods.

127800.explanation: Sufficient shelter is currently arranged. If more is needed, this should be reviewed.

127740.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar focuses on the quality of the relevant executive member, not on whether this person is male or female.

127744.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar is a supporter of entrepreneurs in the broadest sense of the word.

127750.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar strongly supports self-direction regarding the living environment and does not want bans on such matters.

127752.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar supports an organized fireworks show.

127753.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that bulky waste can be dropped off for a fee at the municipal waste disposal sites.

127754.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar supports initiatives around festivals and parties, without imposing additional or higher barriers on entrepreneurs.

127757.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that sport is an important good for its residents. Financial arrangements already exist for welfare recipients, such as the Gelrepas.

127759.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar supports good school buildings, but within the applicable standards (multi-year maintenance plans).

127764.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that the safety of its residents must be guaranteed.

127765.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that the safety of its residents must be guaranteed at known hotspots.

127766.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar is not aware of the need for a shelter for women escaping abuse. If such a need exists, the local VVD Zevenaar is not opposed.

127767.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes it has greater responsibility for housing its own residents than for residents from other municipalities and status holders.

127771.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that welfare recipients should be able to be deployed within their capacities for (socially) meaningful work as part of returning to employment.

127773.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes the income limit should serve as an incentive to motivate welfare recipients to work. After all, working should pay more.

127775.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that electric driving should be stimulated. However, the allocation of charging spots must ensure a proper distribution of parking spaces and should not come at the expense of regular parking spots.

127776.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes it should adhere to nationally established speed limits. However, the local VVD Zevenaar can imagine that speeds on central ring roads could be lowered.

127777.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that care-dependent residents who need care should receive appropriate care, regardless of religion, culture or belief.

127781.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes the current tourist tax is too low. However, the local VVD Zevenaar also believes that the collected tourist tax must flow back to the sector.

127783.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that store opening hours are a matter for the entrepreneur, without the need for municipal steering.

127784.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that every home seeker should have equal opportunities for equal housing. Therefore, no priority groups.

127791.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that better supervision of a legalized coffee shop is preferable to leaving drug trade in criminal hands.

127792.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar supports a proper distribution of housing needs. Zevenaar already has a split of one-third social rental housing, one-third affordable housing, and one-third free sector housing.

127793.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that high-rise buildings and infill development of villages contribute to meeting the housing need.

127794.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that livability within its villages and cores is important. However, when there is no need for a village center, the municipality should not impose or create one.

127795.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes its parking policy is fine as it is. Making parking cheaper means funds must be sourced elsewhere.

127796.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes there is a greater demand for parking than (permit) parking spaces available. The possibilities for this are admittedly mostly on the edge of the center.

127798.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes that residents of Zevenaar benefit from a larger municipality, naturally maintaining their own village and/or city identity.

127800.explanation: The local VVD Zevenaar believes it already meets (or exceeds) the current standards for sheltering family members who join asylum seekers.

127740.explanation: We agree with the statement from our D66 ideology. We will do everything to achieve this and have a female lead candidate. Our alderman has built 600 homes, and we want him to continue with housing construction and his other plans. This is what we focus on in these elections.

127744.explanation: D66 finds it important to be able to buy directly from farmers and supports local purchasing at shops and entrepreneurs. For shops, campsites, and other recreational activities, it must first be checked whether there is no nuisance for others or conflicting interests, such as nature conservation.

127750.explanation: The municipality has a role in encouraging good burning behavior and provides information about this on the website. Wood burning causes as much as 23% of fine particle emissions, which is harmful to yourself, your neighbors, and pets.

127752.explanation: Within certain costs, it is conceivable that the municipality will hold a fireworks show. But must is a strong word; we need to save money.

127753.explanation: It has been agreed that everyone brings bulky waste themselves to the collection station. The waste is processed at the lowest possible cost, fairly calculated via a weighbridge. Free collection, where everyone puts bulky waste on the street at their door, is a step back in time.

127754.explanation: Parties and festivals are part of life. There are sufficient noise standards set, and some festivals provide sound-dampening earplugs. For children's activities, the organizer should critically assess (and listen to) the noise level.

127757.explanation: By using the Gelrepas, the membership fee for a sports club can also be paid. In this way, the municipality contributes to, among other things, promoting sports and participation in society.

127759.explanation: Children have the right to good and healthy school buildings; D66 advocates allocating sufficient funds for this, as these investments lead to better school results. The school boards themselves must ensure timely and proper maintenance of the buildings.

127764.explanation: There are enough BOAs (special enforcement officers) deployed for safety and enforcement on the streets. If more BOAs are needed, the municipality must provide them, but there is no reason for that at the moment.

127765.explanation: Where it is really necessary, effective camera surveillance should be in place. We support this. Simply introducing more camera surveillance does not increase safety. The government must not become Big Brother.

127766.explanation: The assistance for domestic violence (which is not domestic at all) is handled by Veilig Thuis, part of the VGGM Safety Region. D66 closely monitors policy around this issue and will certainly support any application for a women's shelter.

127767.explanation: To a limited extent, housing seekers from the local municipality can be given priority when allocating social rental housing. At D66, everyone has equal rights to a (different) home.

127771.explanation: The era of forced employment is truly behind us. Some customization is possible to offer people a stepping stone and more perspective for an independent future.

127773.explanation: For people with a low income, which would be 0.7% of Zevenaar's residents, there is some income support. This is how we want to prevent worse situations. Poverty is a hidden problem, but as a municipality, we should not conduct income politics. For this reason, for example, there is a food bank.

127775.explanation: The municipality must promote a sustainable vehicle fleet. Residents will not buy an electric car if there is no charging station nearby. D66 wants to encourage electric driving and therefore also more charging stations, including in the villages.

127776.explanation: The livability of neighborhoods and safety for children improves with lower speeds. Speeds in the neighborhoods must really be reduced, and D66 supports a maximum speed of 30 km/h. Only for some through roads can the speed remain 50 km/h.

127777.explanation: Care providers will always have respect for everyone's religious beliefs and cultural practices from their heart. D66 also believes that care providers are open to adapting their behavior to each situation and to the needs and (reasonable) wishes of the care recipient.

127781.explanation: The tourist tax will increase slightly, gradually over the coming years. This tax is currently fairly low compared to neighboring municipalities while we want to offer a high level of facilities to visitors. D66 finds it reasonable to adjust the rate to be more market-conform.

127783.explanation: If retailers would like more shopping Sundays, then that is a decision for the entrepreneurs themselves. D66 will certainly cooperate with this, paying attention to the smaller entrepreneur.

127784.explanation: A refugee with a residence permit did not register in advance in one particular municipality. This person sometimes waits for a year in a small room in an asylum seekers' center. In Zevenaar, 7% is allocated to these refugees. Good distribution across neighborhoods must be ensured.

127791.explanation: The establishment of a coffeeshop is not a goal in itself but can be a solution in certain situations to reduce nuisance. This is currently not an issue.

127792.explanation: For a good balance, 30% social rental housing is a good starting point; this is currently applied by D66 in the development of new neighborhoods and housing projects.

127793.explanation: We need to develop either vertically or horizontally. We cannot build much further outside the city limits. High-rise buildings are feasible in urban areas. With good balconies and consideration for the surroundings, high-rise buildings are an enrichment to the city.

127794.explanation: D66 wants all neighborhoods and villages to have their own meeting place. This could be a village or community center, or a 'MetHen' like in Zevenaar on the Methen. We can improve livability by bringing more facilities to the residents.

127795.explanation: Paid parking in the center and in the parking garage still has a (very) low rate. Because of the parking fees, there is always a parking spot available.

127796.explanation: D66 encourages cycling and public transport use. For shopping in the center, there is sufficient parking space. In the longer term, we will have to handle car usage differently.

127798.explanation: A joint municipality can achieve more for its residents. We can hire more specialists. We often collaborate with Doesburg, Duiven, and Westervoort. We hold our own next to Arnhem. Of course, we must negotiate well, and then we will combine our strengths for all our residents.

127800.explanation: Zevenaar offers sufficient shelter, as mentioned earlier. This means we do not need to set up additional locations. Zevenaar already meets the obligations and we also want to focus on integration. This way, new employees become available for the Dutch economy.

127740.explanation: We strive for a balanced distribution between men and women in the executive board. At the same time, we believe knowledge, experience, and suitability should be decisive in composing the board.

127744.explanation: We support farmers who want to become more sustainable. Small-scale side activities, such as a shop or campground, can facilitate the transition to nature-inclusive farming. Always considering the surroundings and landscape to keep the countryside open and green.

127750.explanation: Clean air and health are paramount for us, especially for children and people with respiratory complaints. Therefore, we believe that in case of a negative burning advisory, wood burning should be limited or temporarily banned.

127752.explanation: We choose a centrally organized show with, for example, lasers and/or drones. This is a safe and clean alternative to consumer fireworks and provides a festive moment for the whole community, with less nuisance for people, animals, and nature.

127753.explanation: Free bulky waste collection for everyone would increase costs for the entire municipality. By properly separating waste at the collection station, we keep the system affordable. For people who cannot do this themselves, customization must remain possible.

127754.explanation: Festivals and parties contribute to vibrancy and social interaction in our municipality. Existing noise standards must be strictly followed, but we do not see a need for further tightening.

127757.explanation: Sports are important for health and social interaction. We believe the municipality should ensure that people on social assistance can participate, even if they cannot pay the membership fee themselves.

127759.explanation: A good school requires a good building. It is the municipality’s responsibility to provide school buildings where safe and pleasant learning is possible. Investing in that is investing in our future.

127764.explanation: Safety is important for everyone. We believe that nuisance and insecurity should primarily be prevented through good prevention, youth work, and social support. Enforcement is sometimes necessary, but more BOAs alone is not the solution in our view.

127765.explanation: It is important that people feel safe in their neighborhood. Camera surveillance is a heavy measure that affects privacy. We prefer visible street presence and preventive measures, and only in exceptional situations temporary camera use.

127766.explanation: If you are not safe at home, there must be a place where you can find protection and support. A women's shelter is therefore indispensable for Zevenaar and the region.

127767.explanation: Local ties can sometimes help keep villages livable but must never lead to exclusion.

127771.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they do not want to work. We believe in trust and good guidance that fits a person’s talents and possibilities. Participation in society is important, but you do not achieve that with coercion, but with support and perspective.

127773.explanation: More and more people with low or middle incomes have difficulty making ends meet but currently fall just outside the arrangements. By raising the income threshold, the municipality can prevent people from getting into trouble.

127775.explanation: More and more residents drive electric cars. By providing sufficient charging points, we make the switch to cleaner transport practical and accessible for everyone.

127776.explanation: We choose 30 km/h in built-up areas because the safety of our residents is paramount. It protects vulnerable road users, such as cycling children and seniors, and makes the neighborhood calmer and healthier. A simple measure for better livability.

127777.explanation: We find it important that people can choose a care provider who matches their background or beliefs as much as possible. Trust between patient and care provider is the basis for good care. This way, we ensure care in which everyone feels welcome and understood.

127781.explanation: The tourist tax has already been increased in recent years. We find it important to keep the recreation sector attractive and affordable and therefore see no reason for a further increase.

127783.explanation: We want to give entrepreneurs the freedom to agree on shopping Sundays, as long as this is voluntary and employees are not pressured. The municipality does not need to regulate this unnecessarily.

127784.explanation: People with a residence permit have the right to safe housing and cannot remain in shelters. For this reason, we continue to prioritize as legally regulated. At the same time, we must increase the housing supply so that starters, seniors, and other home seekers do not become victims.

127791.explanation: We choose for a regulated supply through one coffeeshop, under strict conditions. By organizing sales in a controlled and legal manner, we reduce illegal street trade and lessen safety and nuisance problems. This way, the municipality retains control over the situation and public order.

127792.explanation: Housing is a basic right. We believe that new construction should include at least 30% social rental housing to address the housing shortage. This keeps Zevenaar affordable and accessible for starters, families, and people with lower incomes.

127793.explanation: In the center of Zevenaar, carefully integrated high-rise buildings fit within an urban environment. By building upwards, we preserve space for greenery and prevent further expansion into the surrounding countryside. We consider a height of up to six stories appropriate.

127794.explanation: Village and neighborhood centers are important meeting places and contribute to livability. We believe that larger villages and neighborhoods in Zevenaar should have the possibility of a suitable village or neighborhood center, tailored to the community's needs.

127795.explanation: Parking fees play a role in how we design the city center. By keeping them at a realistic level, we encourage calm and space for pedestrians and cyclists, while keeping Zevenaar’s center accessible to visitors and residents.

127796.explanation: The center of Zevenaar remains welcoming through smart parking choices. We keep parking spaces in the core available for residents and people who really need their car. By encouraging parking on the outskirts and cycling, the center stays accessible, lively, and pleasant.

127798.explanation: A Liemers merger with Doesburg, Duiven, and Westervoort could result in stronger and better-organized care, housing, and services. At the same time, it is important that facilities in the villages are preserved and governance remains recognizable and close to citizens.

127800.explanation: Family reunification is important for a stable start and good integration. Since the IND (Immigration and Naturalisation Service) is located in Zevenaar, we find it logical to temporarily provide extra shelter for family members arriving later where necessary. This is humane, practical, and fits our local responsibility.

127740.explanation: We think it is important to have capable and decisive aldermen in a college. Gender does not matter here.

127744.explanation: Despite the risk of fragmentation, it is acceptable to allow a facility at a farm that is directly connected to the sector. For example, cheese sales at a dairy farm.

127750.explanation: We mainly want to focus on good information campaigns to raise users' awareness of usage and impact. We do not want to be overly paternalistic by imposing bans.

127752.explanation: Our preference is for a New Year’s Eve celebration with a large tent and music.

127753.explanation: Once a month for a fee would be our preference.

127754.explanation: There is a maximum number of decibels that may be produced. Better enforcement of this ensures that this standard is actually applied.

127757.explanation: Sports are important for your health and well-being. Much is already possible in good consultation with clubs.

127759.explanation: That is arranged in the Integral Housing Plan, which sets maintenance and new construction schedules.

127764.explanation: In our opinion, there are currently enough enforcement officers.

127765.explanation: Due to the privacy of our residents, we are against camera surveillance. We have always been against it. It is better to deploy more enforcement officers or police where necessary. This is already happening.

127766.explanation: A full-fledged shelter requires specialized care, security, and continuity. This is more effectively organized at a regional level.

127767.explanation: We want residents of the municipality of Zevenaar to be able to continue living in their own municipality. This also helps counteract aging populations.

127771.explanation: People often end up on social assistance unintentionally. Good guidance toward a workplace is preferred but not always possible. The same applies to socially meaningful work.

127773.explanation: Often this is already done when possible and assessed per situation. We have prevention goals for this. Poverty is so damaging to health that we really must try to prevent it.

127775.explanation: This is already happening and is also part of making new plans. The allocation of locations for charging stations must align with the number of electric cars in the municipality.

127776.explanation: It makes no sense for our municipality. Speed limits have already been imposed where necessary.

127777.explanation: This is our preference but unfortunately not always possible.

127781.explanation: Our tourist tax is low compared to the regional average. This also means that the municipality must pay more attention to tourism to continue attracting visitors.

127783.explanation: A uniform policy among downtown retailers would help more. First, have all shops open on the current shopping Sundays, then evaluate. For small entrepreneurs, keeping their shops open is already a challenge.

127784.explanation: We cannot change that. It is regulated by law in the 2014 Housing Act imposed by the government.

127791.explanation: It does not seem like a good plan to have a coffeeshop in Zevenaar for safety reasons.

127792.explanation: The council focused on this a few years ago. Unfortunately, it is not always complied with.

127793.explanation: Building up to 4 stories high is fine. Higher is not.

127794.explanation: The problem is often that there are not enough volunteers found for this.

127795.explanation: Various pilots have shown that this does not result in more visitors to the center.

127796.explanation: Research results indicate that parking spaces are more often underutilized than overutilized.

127798.explanation: Scaling up can lead to a stronger voice in provincial consultations or obtaining subsidies. But if not all municipalities are willing, postponement is better. Support both within Zevenaar and the other municipalities is a prerequisite for success.

127800.explanation: For now, our municipality does enough in asylum reception. We already shelter these late arrivals within our municipality. From a social perspective, late arrivals should be accommodated and then fairly distributed across the Netherlands.

127740.explanation: No, it concerns suitability for the function. This is independent of the candidates' gender.

127744.explanation: Our rural area is rich in opportunities. WijZZ wants agricultural entrepreneurs to maintain prospects and have space to innovate sustainably. But whether this is desirable at a particular location strongly depends on the situation.

127750.explanation: The municipality of Zevenaar is a participant in the 'Clean Air Agreement'. This creates awareness for responsible burning. WijZZ will continue to support this.

127752.explanation: No, because currently this is being considered by the national government to allow small-scale fireworks displays by, for example, neighborhood associations. According to us, a large municipal fireworks show does not work.

127753.explanation: WijZZ sees no reason to change the current regulations.

127754.explanation: The regulations and enforcement regarding this are well and adequately arranged. WijZZ sees no need to adjust this.

127757.explanation: WijZZ wholeheartedly supports this scheme. WijZZ's principle is that everyone counts. This scheme gives effect to that.

127759.explanation: Certainly, WijZZ chooses to invest in high-quality and healthy school buildings.

127764.explanation: The role of enforcement officers (boa's) grows when police capacity is unavailable. Safety remains an important priority for WijZZ.

127765.explanation: However, WijZZ prefers to opt for occasional use of camera surveillance for issues such as bicycle thefts or persistent nuisance in certain areas.

127766.explanation: Domestic violence must be fought firmly. But a women's shelter is a regional facility. In cases of domestic violence, WijZZ chooses to remove the perpetrators from the home instead of the victim.

127767.explanation: Absolutely! WijZZ prioritizes village ties as a strong argument for housing allocation. Starters and seniors who have lived for several years in a village or center deserve priority for the allocation of a social rental home in that village or center.

127771.explanation: WijZZ believes this cannot be mandated. Volunteer work in the local environment can, however, be more actively encouraged.

127773.explanation: The municipality of Zevenaar has a generous poverty policy. In addition, WijZZ opts for active poverty reduction for its residents. Tackling hidden poverty is also a priority for WijZZ.

127775.explanation: Zevenaar suffers from parking shortages. Reserving special places that are not used or occupied daily is undesirable. According to WijZZ, the best option is to smartly look for places where they can be located.

127776.explanation: WijZZ stands for traffic safety. This is therefore a good measure to make the already busy streets safer.

127777.explanation: People must receive the care they need. According to WijZZ, the range of care providers in our municipality is aimed at meeting these demands.

127781.explanation: Tourist tax must keep pace with municipalities in the region. This keeps the municipality attractive for recreation. The rate in the municipality of Zevenaar meets this. WijZZ currently chooses not to increase it.

127783.explanation: WijZZ chooses to leave this as much as possible to the shop entrepreneurs themselves.

127784.explanation: WijZZ opts to follow national policy here. But in housing shortages, every extra priority puts additional pressure on available homes. This is undesirable.

127791.explanation: A coffeeshop facility is not desired on the scale of Zevenaar. This unnecessarily causes nuisance to the surroundings.

127792.explanation: WijZZ supports the principles of the Housing Deal 2.0 in which this is included. So at least 30% social rental homes in larger housing projects in the municipality of Zevenaar.

127793.explanation: Living space is becoming increasingly limited. This compels us to be innovative and smarter with our available public space. WijZZ therefore does not exclude taller residential buildings from the outset.

127794.explanation: WijZZ is in favor of facilities close to residents. But this does not mean it must be arranged in village centers.

127795.explanation: WijZZ sees no reason to change this policy line. Research has shown that making parking cheaper does not lead to visitors staying longer in the center.

127796.explanation: The number of parking spaces must match the number of users. WijZZ sees this as a bottleneck in our municipality. Therefore, WijZZ's priority is to examine per situation/area what is possible to adjust current parking standards.

127798.explanation: Reorganization? 'No, unless'. Only if it demonstrably benefits the residents of Zevenaar. No merger without firm guarantees: retention of facilities, decision-making power, and local identity.

127800.explanation: The municipality of Zevenaar provides for this. This ensures that family members who must register with the IND in Zevenaar do not have to travel across the country.

127740.explanation: We are not opposed to this, but aldermen must be selected based on quality.

127744.explanation: The municipality must consider each case to determine if this is desirable. Not all situations are suitable for a shop or campsite.

127750.explanation: We do not believe there should be a total ban, but stricter rules must be introduced and enforced.

127752.explanation: We have no objection to this, but it should not be mandatory.

127753.explanation: We are open to alternatives, such as a once-a-year collection day or small-scale collection actions in neighborhoods or centers.

127754.explanation: Existing noise standards must be enforced.

127757.explanation: It is good for the people and good for the associations.

127759.explanation: The Integrated Housing Plan was recently adopted, outlining which schools the municipality will invest in over the coming years. We see no reason to change this now.

127764.explanation: It is the primary task of the police. They should receive more funding to carry out their duties.

127765.explanation: At hotspots regarding risk and safety areas, but not on every street corner.

127766.explanation: We have a regional function. It is important that there are enough places for people who need to access them. If that must be in Zevenaar, then we are open to that.

127767.explanation: For part of the offered rental homes, people from the municipality itself must get priority.

127771.explanation: Not everyone is capable of performing socially meaningful work.

127773.explanation: Not all assistance depends on income thresholds. We find it important that people are helped early so they do not fall into poverty or debts.

127775.explanation: There are sufficient places to charge an electric car.

127776.explanation: This must be assessed on a case-by-case basis; if the speed increase contributes to safety, we are open to it.

127777.explanation: Everyone always has a choice regarding which healthcare provider they prefer.

127781.explanation: Tourist tax should be in line with the market.

127784.explanation: Priority for people seeking housing lies with the housing corporations.

127791.explanation: We are not opposed to this.

127792.explanation: We prefer 35%, given the shortage in the housing market.

127793.explanation: We are not opposed to this, provided it is appropriate and desirable in the area.

127794.explanation: A village center has an important role in livability.

127795.explanation: Not cheaper, but not necessarily more expensive either.

127796.explanation: There simply is no room for that.

127798.explanation: The merging of municipalities is not a goal in itself. It is important that the municipality of Zevenaar makes choices that are best for its own residents.

127800.explanation: The municipality of Zevenaar has already created extra space.

statements.title: Zevenaar should merge with the municipalities of Doesburg, Duiven and Westervoort.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Zevenaar should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Paid parking in the centre of Zevenaar should become cheaper.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
All larger villages in the municipality should have their own village centre.
Zevenaar should have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Zevenaar should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Zevenaar moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Zevenaar.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed in Zevenaar.

titleParts.text: In Zevenaar moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality of Zevenaar should allow more high-rise residential blocks.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More parking spaces should be provided in the centre of Zevenaar.
The municipality should provide extra space to temporarily accommodate family members joining asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zorgen voor extra ruimte om nareizende familieleden van

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
